title: This tools shows you how much you spend on tokens...
summary: NeuralNine presents a tool designed to help users track and calculate their token expenditure costs. The tool appears to assist developers in managing API or LLM usage expenses.
writeup: cc-usage is an open-source CLI tool that reads locally cached session data from AI coding agent CLIs (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode) and reports token consumption with projected API costs, even when running under a subscription. The primary use case is subscription ROI analysis: seeing what API spending would have been without a flat-rate plan makes the upgrade-or-cancel decision concrete and data-driven. The video walks through provider filtering, daily and monthly breakdowns, session-level and 5-hour-block views, date range slicing, and a JSON output flag for scripting.
key_points: cc-usage projects token usage into USD at API rates regardless of subscription, so a user paying a flat monthly fee can directly compare what they consumed against what it would have cost on pay-per-token.
Data is machine-local only: the tool reads whichever agent's local cache exists on that specific machine, so usage across multiple devices or cloud environments is not aggregated.
Filtering by provider is done by passing the agent name as a positional argument (e.g., cc-usage claude or cc-usage codex), and granularity switches between daily, monthly, and session views.
Claude Code's 5-hour rolling billing blocks are surfaced with cc-usage claude blocks, exposing per-block cost for users who need to understand session-window economics.
Date range filtering uses --since and --until flags with YYYY-MM-DD dates, enabling arbitrary time slices for any view.
The --json flag serializes the full result as a JSON object, making cc-usage composable with scripts and automation pipelines.
